name: Cantina Giuseppe Rinaldi
blurb: After Beppe Rinaldi died in 2018, daughters Marta and Carlotta took over a winery that remains one of the ideological anchors of traditional Barolo, with long macerations, no new oak, and no concessions to fashion. The visit is genuinely personal rather than staged; you are in the cellar with the people who make the wine, and the conversation reflects it. Because their bottles do not show up at retail without a wait list, tasting them at the source is the practical route anyway.
